reportOverview.reportTitle: Insights for Food Response and Nutrient Sensitivity
sectionsUnavailable: No direct Insights for Food Response and Nutrient Sensitivity sample report has been captured.
Sibling Food Sensitivity and Healthy Nutrition sample rows are used only as observed output-structure evidence.
No calibrated food-response, nutrient-sensitivity, micronutrient, supplement, symptom, diet-history, lab, metabolic, or ancestry applicability model is supplied with this fixture.
reportOverview.plainEnglishMeaning: This run can describe supplied lactose-related marker context and reproduce sibling sample output structure, but it cannot diagnose food reactions, nutrient deficiency, allergies, celiac disease, symptoms, metabolic disease, or recommend diet or supplements.

resultRows.groupTitle: Food response and nutrient sensitivity context
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived MCM6/LCT rs4988235 CT call is available for lactase-persistence context. This is not a diagnosis, symptom explanation, food-sensitivity result, diet recommendation, or supplement recommendation.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: This marker can provide lactose-related context, but symptoms, food history, diet history, labs, environment, medications, ancestry, and professional review matter for nutrition decisions.

resultRows.item: Food response and nutrient sensitivity model
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: No calibrated food-response, nutrient-sensitivity, micronutrient, supplement, symptom, diet-history, lab, metabolic, or ancestry applicability model is supplied with this fixture.
genes: Food response and nutrient sensitivity model
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: The report can describe supplied marker context and unavailable sections, but it should not make diet, supplement, food-response, nutrient, metabolic, symptom, lab, or diagnosis conclusions without validated evidence.

resultRows.item: Alcohol, caffeine, lactose, and gluten summary cards
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample summary cards show Alcohol: Slow Metabolism; Caffeine: Fast Metabolism; Lactose: Likely Sensitive; Gluten: Not Likely Sensitive, with a legend separating likely-sensitive from not-likely-sensitive labels.
resultRows.description: The sample summary page presents the four result cards before detailed substance and sensitivity sections.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sibling Food Sensitivity sample-report structure only. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not a personal alcohol, caffeine, lactose, gluten, allergy, celiac, symptom, diet, supplement, medical, or treatment recommendation.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample introduces alcohol and caffeine as substances whose metabolism can vary with genetic markers, then notes that slower metabolism markers may mean the body takes longer to clear alcohol or caffeine.
resultRows.description: The substances section precedes the detailed Alcohol Metabolism and Caffeine Metabolism pages.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sibling Food Sensitivity sample-report structure only. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not a personal alcohol, caffeine, lactose, gluten, allergy, celiac, symptom, diet, supplement, medical, or treatment recommendation.

resultRows.item: ADH1C gene table and slow-metabolism result
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample Alcohol Metabolism page says the sample genome is more likely to have slow alcohol metabolism. The visible gene table lists ADH1C marker GP000071 genotype TT with slow alcohol metabolism impact, plus ADH1C GP000072 genotype CC and ADH1C GP000073 genotype TT with normal alcohol metabolism impact.
resultRows.description: The page also lists common alcohol sources and a what-can-you-do block about tracking drinks, spacing them out, and drinking water.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sibling Food Sensitivity sample-report structure only. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not a personal alcohol, caffeine, lactose, gluten, allergy, celiac, symptom, diet, supplement, medical, or treatment recommendation.

resultRows.item: CYP1A2 gene table and fast-metabolism result
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample Caffeine Metabolism page says the sample genome is more likely to have fast caffeine metabolism. The visible gene table lists CYP1A2 marker GP000071 genotype TT with fast caffeine metabolism impact.
resultRows.description: The page lists common caffeine sources such as coffee, tea, soft drinks, energy drinks, chocolate, certain medications, and yerba mate, plus a sleep-timing action block.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sibling Food Sensitivity sample-report structure only. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not a personal alcohol, caffeine, lactose, gluten, allergy, celiac, symptom, diet, supplement, medical, or treatment recommendation.

resultRows.item: Food sensitivity versus food allergy explanation
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample Sensitivities section distinguishes food sensitivity from food allergy, explains that sensitivities can involve difficulty digesting components such as lactose or gluten, and names non-genetic contributors such as gut microbiome imbalance, gastrointestinal disease, stress, age, additives, sleep, and physical inactivity.
resultRows.description: The section explicitly says genetics can play a role but do not work in isolation.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sibling Food Sensitivity sample-report structure only. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not a personal alcohol, caffeine, lactose, gluten, allergy, celiac, symptom, diet, supplement, medical, or treatment recommendation.

resultRows.item: HLA-DQA1 and HLA-DQ8 gene table with not-likely-sensitive result
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample Gluten Sensitivity page says the sample genome is not likely sensitive to gluten. The visible gene table lists HLA-DQA1 marker GP000071 genotype TT and HLA-DQ8 marker GP000072 genotype CC, both with not-likely-sensitive-to-gluten impact.
resultRows.description: The page lists common gluten sources and includes a what-can-you-do block about a temporary gluten-removal trial.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sibling Food Sensitivity sample-report structure only. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not a personal alcohol, caffeine, lactose, gluten, allergy, celiac, symptom, diet, supplement, medical, or treatment recommendation.

resultRows.item: LCT and MCM6-LCT gene table with likely-sensitive result
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample Lactose Sensitivity page says the sample genome is more likely to be sensitive to lactose. The visible gene table lists LCT marker GP000071 genotype TT with more-likely-sensitive-to-lactose impact and MCM6-LCT marker GP000072 genotype CC with less-likely-sensitive-to-lactose impact.
resultRows.description: The page lists common lactose sources and a what-can-you-do block about tracking dairy reactions, considering lower-lactose foods, and discussing dairy reduction or lactose enzymes with a healthcare practitioner.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sibling Food Sensitivity sample-report structure only. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not a personal alcohol, caffeine, lactose, gluten, allergy, celiac, symptom, diet, supplement, medical, or treatment recommendation.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: Caffeine: common result AA; sample user result AA; impact: Normal breakdown of caffeine.
resultRows.description: The public sample page 6 labels this trait NORMAL; 53% of the population shares this displayed genetic risk or set-up.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sibling Healthy Nutrition sample-report structure only. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not a personal food-reaction, nutrient-status, deficiency, diet, supplement, symptom, lab, metabolic, or treatment recommendation.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: Lactose Intolerance: common result AA; sample user result GG; impact: Lactose intolerance expected.
resultRows.description: The public sample page 7 labels this trait HIGH RISK; 9% of the population shares this displayed genetic risk or set-up.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sibling Healthy Nutrition sample-report structure only. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not a personal food-reaction, nutrient-status, deficiency, diet, supplement, symptom, lab, metabolic, or treatment recommendation.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: Gluten Sensitivity: common result CC; TT; sample user result CC; TT; impact: Low risk for sensitivity.
resultRows.description: The public sample page 8 labels this trait LOW; 56% of the population shares this displayed genetic risk or set-up.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sibling Healthy Nutrition sample-report structure only. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not a personal food-reaction, nutrient-status, deficiency, diet, supplement, symptom, lab, metabolic, or treatment recommendation.

resultRows.description: The public sample page 9 labels this trait AVERAGE; 87% of the population shares this displayed genetic risk or set-up.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sibling Healthy Nutrition sample-report structure only. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not a personal food-reaction, nutrient-status, deficiency, diet, supplement, symptom, lab, metabolic, or treatment recommendation.

resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sibling Healthy Nutrition sample-report structure only. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not a personal food-reaction, nutrient-status, deficiency, diet, supplement, symptom, lab, metabolic, or treatment recommendation.

foodResponseContext: Use this report as educational food-response and nutrient-sensitivity genetics context, not as an allergy, sensitivity, intolerance, or deficiency diagnosis.
Treat sibling sample rows as output-format examples only; they are not direct Insights sample rows and not local findings.
Discuss symptoms, food history, diet history, labs, allergy evaluation, nutrient questions, medications, and health goals with qualified professionals.
Do not eliminate foods, start supplements, interpret labs, or change treatment based on genotype alone.

missingInputs: Direct Insights mock-report sections and rows have not been captured.
No calibrated food-response, nutrient-sensitivity, micronutrient, supplement, symptom, diet-history, lab, metabolic, or ancestry applicability model is supplied with this fixture.
Broader symptoms, food history, diet history, labs, medications, medical history, ancestry applicability, environment, and professional assessment are not represented in this fixture.
uncertainty: Nutrition, food response, nutrient status, symptoms, and metabolic outcomes depend on genetics plus symptoms, diet history, labs, medical history, medications, environment, ancestry applicability, and professional review.
A single marker or sibling sample row should not be used to diagnose food reactions, nutrient deficiency, metabolic disease, or to prescribe diet or supplement changes.
limitations: This validation result uses derived evidence only; raw genome data is not included.
Sibling sample rows are output-structure evidence only, not direct Insights sample rows and not local genotype-derived findings.

The report is educational and does not diagnose food allergy, food sensitivity, celiac disease, lactose intolerance, nutrient deficiency, metabolic disease, prescribe diets, recommend supplements, interpret labs, or provide treatment guidance.
